summary: Tooling-only PR updating `tools/wiki_tu.py` to replace hard-coded wiki table column numbers with a `ColumnIndex` IntEnum and to use named indices for file, Discord, and GitHub columns when reading assignee data.

key_files.role: Changed Python tooling script that parses wiki translation-unit table rows for assignee metadata.

what_changed.text: Added `ColumnIndex` IntEnum with named entries for table columns: `FILE`, `MATCHED`, `TOTAL`, `REMAINING`, `TEXT_PERCENT`, `DATA_PERCENT`, `DISCORD`, and `GITHUB`.
Replaced literal column indices `5`, `6`, and `0` in `read_tr` with `ColumnIndex.DISCORD`, `ColumnIndex.GITHUB`, and `ColumnIndex.FILE` respectively.

smart_moves.text: Named enum values make the wiki table layout explicit and reduce the chance of future off-by-one or stale-column mistakes in this parser.

decomp_lessons.text: For project tooling that parses structured progress tables, prefer named column constants/enums over unexplained numeric indices so table-layout assumptions remain reviewable.

naming_conventions.text: Column indices were represented by a `ColumnIndex` IntEnum using uppercase member names matching table concepts, including `FILE`, `DISCORD`, and `GITHUB`.

follow_up_queries: wiki_tu.py read_tr discord github column index

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use named column indices in wiki table tooling
accepted_candidate_records.text: When parsing fixed-layout wiki TU tables in tooling, use named column constants or an enum rather than bare numeric indices. PR 2651 introduced `ColumnIndex` in `tools/wiki_tu.py` and replaced direct uses of `td[0]`, `td[5]`, and `td[6]` with `ColumnIndex.FILE`, `ColumnIndex.DISCORD`, and `ColumnIndex.GITHUB`.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting wiki TU table column order for tooling maintainers
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2651 encoded the expected wiki TU table columns in `ColumnIndex`: file, matched, total, remaining, text percent, data percent, Discord, and GitHub. Curator may consider routing this as a documentation or tooling-maintainer note if a source-owned table schema document exists.
source_update_candidates.reason: The diff exposes an implicit table schema, but the PR slice does not identify an owning documentation file, so this should remain a candidate rather than an accepted path fact or global standard.
